H.use: Fuel for rockets and clean energy applications
Steel and metal welding, reducing metal ores

He.use: Cooling superconducting magnets in MRI machines
Pressurization of spacecraft and rocket fuel tanks

Be.use: Aircraft components due to high strength-to-weight ratio

B.use: Borosilicate glass for laboratory and kitchenware

C.use: Carbon steel production (90% of all steel)
Coal for power generation and heating

O.use: Essential for all fuel burning and welding

F.use: Fluorinated drugs and toothpaste fluoride compounds

Ne.use: Neon signs and high-voltage indicator lamps
Helium-neon gas lasers for scientific and industrial use

Na.use: Sodium hydroxide (caustic soda) in paper and textile production
Sodium chloride for salt production and food preservation
Soda ash (sodium carbonate) in glass manufacturing

Al.use: Electrical transmission cables due to high conductivity

Si.use: Semiconductor material for computer chips and solar cells

P.use: Phosphorus compounds in detergents and flame retardants

S.use: Vulcanization of rubber for tires and gaskets

Cl.use: Disinfection of drinking water and swimming pools
Hydrochloric acid and other industrial chemicals

Ar.use: Inert shielding gas for MIG and TIG arc welding
Filler gas in incandescent and fluorescent lamps
Thermal insulation filling for double-glazed windows

K.use: Potassium compounds as essential plant nutrients
Potassium hydroxide in soap and detergent production
Potassium salts for treating potassium deficiency

Ca.use: Limestone and calcium carbonate in cement and concrete

Sc.use: Aluminum-scandium alloys for aerospace and sports equipment
Metal halide lamps for film and stadium lighting

Ti.use: Jet engines and airframes for high strength-to-weight ratio
Biocompatible surgical implants and dental fixtures
Titanium dioxide as white pigment in paints and plastics

V.use: Ferrovanadium additive for high-strength structural steels
Vanadium pentoxide catalyst in sulfuric acid production
Vanadium redox flow batteries for grid energy storage

Cr.use: Chromium for corrosion resistance in steel
Chromium compounds as pigments and dyes

Mn.use: Ferromanganese additive to remove oxygen and sulfur in steelmaking
Manganese dioxide cathode in alkaline and dry-cell batteries
Hadfield steel for railway tracks and heavy-duty wear parts

Fe.use: Primary component of steel (about 99% of global metal production)

Co.use: Cobalt in high-strength super-alloys for jet engines

Ni.use: Nickel plating for protective and decorative coating

Cu.use: Electrical wiring and power distribution cables
Water and heating pipes in buildings

Zn.use: Brass alloy for decorative and functional items
Zinc supplements for immune system support

Ga.use: Gallium arsenide and gallium nitride semiconductors for LEDs and RF chips
High-efficiency multi-junction solar cells for space applications

Ge.use: Infrared optics for thermal imaging and night-vision systems
Germanium-doped core glass in optical fiber

As.use: Gallium arsenide wafers for RF and optoelectronic devices
Hardening additive for lead-acid battery grids and ammunition
Chromated copper arsenate for industrial timber (restricted use)

Se.use: Photoreceptor drums in photocopiers and laser printers
Decolorizer and red pigment in specialty glass
Selenium supplements and animal feed additive

Br.use: Bromine compounds in fire-resistant textiles and foams

Kr.use: High-efficiency krypton-filled incandescent and photographic flash lamps
Krypton fluoride excimer lasers for lithography and research
Thermal insulation gas in high-performance window glazing

Rb.use: Rubidium atomic clocks for GPS and telecommunications timing
Laser cooling experiments and Bose-Einstein condensate studies
Rubidium-82 PET imaging tracer for cardiac perfusion

Sr.use: Strontium salts provide red color in fireworks and flares
Strontium ferrite in ceramic permanent magnets

Y.use: Yttrium aluminum garnet (YAG) hosts for solid-state lasers
Red phosphor in LED and legacy CRT displays
YBCO high-temperature superconductor wires and tapes

Zr.use: Zircaloy fuel-rod cladding in nuclear reactors
Zirconia refractory ceramics and cubic zirconia gemstones
Zirconium alloys for dental crowns and joint implants

Nb.use: Niobium-titanium magnets in MRI and particle accelerators
Microalloying element for high-strength low-alloy pipeline steels
Niobium capacitors and thin-film electronic components

Mo.use: Molybdenum alloys for high-temperature and corrosion resistance

Pd.use: Catalytic converters for automotive emission control
Multilayer ceramic capacitors and plating for connectors
Palladium membranes for hydrogen purification and storage

Ag.use: Silver contacts in switches and circuit boards
Silver halide in photographic film (legacy use)
Sterling silver for decorative and valuable items

Cd.use: Cadmium sulfide pigments for bright yellow and orange paints

In.use: Indium tin oxide (ITO) transparent coatings for touch screens
Low-melting indium solders for cryogenic and vacuum seals
Indium compounds in LEDs, laser diodes and photovoltaics

Sn.use: Tin-lead solder for joining electronic components
Tin plating to prevent corrosion of steel
Bronze alloys for decorative objects and bells

Sb.use: Antimony trioxide synergist in plastic and textile flame retardants
Antimony-lead grids in automotive lead-acid batteries
Hardening additive in type metal, pewter and ammunition alloys

Te.use: Machinability additive for free-cutting steel and copper
Thermoelectric cooling and phase-change memory materials

I.use: Iodine disinfectant for wounds and skin sterilization
Iodized salt for preventing iodine deficiency diseases
Silver iodide in photographic and cloud seeding

Xe.use: Xenon arc and HID lamps for cinema projectors and automotive headlights
Xenon ion thrusters for satellite propulsion
Xenon anesthesia and hyperpolarized xenon MRI imaging

Cs.use: Cesium atomic clocks define the SI unit of the second
Cesium formate high-density drilling and completion fluids
Cesium photocathodes in night-vision and photomultiplier tubes

Ba.use: Barite (barium sulfate) weighting agent in drilling muds
Barium titanate in ceramic capacitors and piezoelectric sensors
Barium sulfate contrast agent for gastrointestinal X-rays

W.use: High-strength tungsten in aerospace and military

Pt.use: Platinum catalysts in chemical industry and vehicles
Platinum for luxury items and watches

Au.use: Gold for luxury and decorative items
Gold plating on circuit boards and connectors

Hg.use: Mercury vapor in fluorescent and high-intensity discharge lamps
Chlor-alkali process for chlorine and sodium hydroxide (being phased out)

Pb.use: Lead barriers in X-ray and nuclear facilities
Lead compounds as pigments (restricted in modern use)

Bi.use: Bismuth subsalicylate in antacid and antidiarrheal medicines
Low-melting fusible alloys for fire sprinklers and fuses
Bismuth oxychloride pigment for pearlescent finishes

Th.use: Thorium-232 fuel in next-generation nuclear reactors
Thoriated tungsten electrodes for TIG welding
Thorium compounds in catalysts and refractories

U.use: Uranium enrichment for research and medical isotopes
